summary: Splice removed [a,b] spans out of a baked video by trim+concat re-encode — the render_final quick-trim dedup MECHANIC, not the edit DECISION.

parity.note: Deterministic for a fixed ffmpeg build: the trim+concat filtergraph is a pure function of the removed spans (and the source's own duration), and libx264 at a pinned preset/crf over identical input bytes emits identical output bytes. tests/test_op_edit_splice.py runs the handler twice on one clip and sha256-compares the bytes, and pins the filtergraph string, so a drift shows as a hash/argv diff rather than as mystery-different pixels. WHICH spans to remove is the FE's editorial decision and arrives as params (data); the overlay-timeline remap around it (broll/motion/fx times) is arithmetic that stays on the brain in apply_trims.py — only this splice crosses.

spans.description: The removed [a,b] spans on the SOURCE clock — merged and non-overlapping, from the FE dedup marks. WHICH spans to cut is the decision and it arrives as data; how they become a trim+concat filtergraph is the handler's. These are spans to REMOVE, never a threshold.
